title: Radiation dose reduction and improved consistency between sessions in hybrid imaging studies
abstract: In a multi-session imaging study, information from a previous imaging session is stored in a Binary Large Object (BLOB). Current emission imaging data are reconstructed into a non-attenuation corrected (NAC) current emission image. A spatial transform is generated aligning a previous NAC emission image from the BLOB to the current NAC emission image. A previous computed tomography (CT) image from the BLOB is warped using the spatial transform, and the current emission imaging data are reconstructed with attenuation correction using the warped CT image. Alternatively, low dose current emission imaging data and a current CT image are acquired, a spatial transform is generated aligning the previous CT image to the current CT image, a previous attenuation corrected (AC) emission image from the BLOB is warped using the spatial transform, and the current emission imaging data are reconstructed using the current CT image with the warped AC emission image as prior.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. An imaging controller (10) comprising an electronic processor (12) programmed to perform an imaging method executing a current imaging session (102, 202) which is part of a multi-session imaging study of a subject, the imaging method including: retrieving imaging data acquisition and reconstruction parameters of a previous imaging session (100) of the imaging study from a Binary Large OBject (BLOB) (300) that stores information for the imaging study including at least the imaging data acquisition and reconstruction parameters of the previous imaging session; automatically configuring an imaging system (6, 8) with the retrieved imaging data acquisition and reconstruction parameters of the previous imaging session; after the automatic configuring, controlling the imaging system to acquire current imaging data (124, 160, 220, 224) from the subject and to reconstruct the current imaging data to generate a current image (126) of the subject; and storing the imaging data acquisition and reconstruction parameters used in the controlling in the BLOB as the imaging data acquisition and reconstruction parameters of the current imaging session (102, 202) of the imaging study. 2. The imaging controller (10) of claim 1 wherein the imaging method further includes: retrieving spatial information for a tumor or lesion finding of the previous imaging session (100); computing a reduced scan range for the current imaging session as compared with the previous imaging session using the spatial information for the tumor or lesion finding of the previous imaging session; and prior to the controlling, automatically configuring the imaging system (6, 8) with the z position minimum and maximum plan lines for the current imaging session to implement the reduced scan range for the current imaging session. 3. The imaging controller (10) of any one of claims 1-2 wherein the imaging method further includes: 

 after the automatic configuring and before the controlling, receiving at least one change to the automatic configuration of the imaging system (6, 8), wherein the controlling is performed with the retrieved imaging data acquisition and reconstruction parameters of the previous imaging session modified by the received at least one change. 4. The imaging controller (10) of any one of claims 1-3 wherein the retrieving further includes retrieving spatial parameters of at least one finding of the previous imaging session (100) from the BLOB (300) and the imaging method further includes: using the retrieved spatial parameters of the at least one finding to identify a region of interest (ROI) or delineation contour in the current image (126). 5. The imaging controller (10) of claim 4 wherein the imaging method further includes: adjusting the ROI or delineation contour in the current image (126) using a region growing or contour adjustment algorithm; wherein the storing includes storing the adjusted ROI or delineation contour in the BLOB (300). 6. The imaging controller (10) of any one of claims 1-5 wherein the current imaging data includes current emission imaging data (124) and the imaging method further includes: reconstructing the current emission imaging data without attenuation correction to generate a non-attenuation corrected current emission image (126) of the subject; retrieving a previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) and a previous computed tomography (CT) image (112) acquired in the previous imaging session (100) from the BLOB (300); generating a spatial transform (130) to spatially align the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image to the current non-attenuation corrected emission image of the subject; warping the previous CT image using the spatial transform to generate a warped CT image (142); and 

 reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the warped CT image to generate an attenuation-corrected current emission image of the subject (148, 168). 7. The imaging controller (10) of any one of claims 1-5 wherein the current imaging data includes current emission imaging data (224) and current computed tomography (CT) imaging data (220) of the subject and the imaging method further includes: retrieving a previous CT image (112) and a previous attenuation corrected emission image (118) acquired in the previous imaging session (100) from the BLOB (300); reconstructing the current CT imaging data to generate a current CT image (222); generating a spatial transform (230) to spatially align the previous CT image to the current CT image of the subject; warping the previous attenuation corrected emission image using the spatial transform to generate a warped attenuation corrected emission image (234); and reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the current CT image and using the warped attenuation corrected emission image as prior data to generate an attenuation-corrected current emission image (238) of the subject. 8. An imaging controller (10) comprising an electronic processor (12) programmed to perform a reduced radiation dose imaging method including: acquiring current emission imaging data (124) from a subject using an emission imaging system (6); reconstructing the current emission imaging data without attenuation correction to generate a non-attenuation corrected current emission image (126) of the subject; generating a spatial transform (130) to spatially align a previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) to the current non-attenuation corrected emission image of the subject; warping a previous computed tomography (CT) image (112) that is spatially aligned with the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image using the spatial transform to generate a warped CT image (142); and 

 reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the warped CT image to generate an attenuation-corrected current emission image of the subject (148, 168). 9. The imaging controller of claim 8 wherein the operation of reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction comprises: reconstructing the current emission imaging data (124) with attenuation correction performed using the warped CT image (142) as an attenuation map. 10. The imaging controller of claim 9 further comprising one of: scaling the previous CT image (112) before the warping to adjust for a difference between particle energy of the emission data and particle energy of the x-rays; and prior to using the warped CT image (142) as an attenuation map, scaling the warped CT image to adjust for the difference between particle energy of the emission data and particle energy of the x-rays. 11. The imaging controller of claim 8 wherein the operation of reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the warped CT image includes: acquiring low dose CT imaging data (160) of the subject using a CT imaging system (8) with an x-ray dose that is lower than the x-ray dose that was used to acquire the previous CT image (112); reconstructing the low dose CT imaging data using the warped CT image (142) as prior information to generate a low dose CT image (162) of the subject; and reconstructing the current emission imaging data (124) with attenuation correction performed using the low dose CT image (162) as an attenuation map. 12. The imaging controller of claim 8 wherein the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) is of the subject and the operation of reconstructing the current emission imaging data (124) with attenuation correction performed using the warped CT image (142) includes: computing a CT reusability metric using at least one of (1) weight change of the subject between a time of acquisition of the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image of the subject and the time of acquisition of the current emission imaging data from the 

 subject and (2) a relative overlap metric between the current non-attenuation corrected emission image (126) of the subject and the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) spatially aligned using the spatial transformation (130); and based on the CT reusability metric performing one of: (i) reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the warped CT image (142) as an attenuation map; and (ii) acquiring low dose CT imaging data (160) of the subject using a CT imaging system (8) with an x-ray dose that is lower than the x-ray dose that was used to acquire the previous CT image, reconstructing the low dose CT imaging data using the warped CT image (142) as prior information to generate a low dose CT image (162) of the subject, and reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the low dose CT image as an attenuation map. 13. The imaging controller of any one of claims 8-12 wherein the emission imaging is positron emission tomography (PET) or single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) imaging. 14. The imaging controller of any one of claims 8-13 wherein the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) is of the subject and the previous CT image (112) is of the subject. 15. The imaging controller of any one of claims 8-13 wherein the reduced radiation dose imaging method further includes: retrieving the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) and the previous CT image (112) that is spatially aligned with the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image from an emission/CT study atlas. 16. An imaging system comprising: an emission imaging system (6) comprising a positron emission tomography (PET) or single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) system; a computed tomography (CT) imaging system (8); and 

 an imaging controller (10) as set forth in any one of claims 8-15 configured to acquire the current emission imaging data (124) from the subject using the emission imaging system and to acquire CT imaging data (160) using the CT imaging system. 17. A non-transitory storage medium storing instructions readable and executable by a combined emission imaging system (6) and computed tomography (CT) imaging system (8) to perform a reduced radiation dose imaging method including: acquiring current emission imaging data (124) from a subject using the emission imaging system; reconstructing the current emission imaging data without attenuation correction to generate a non-attenuation corrected current emission image (126) of the subject; generating a spatial transform (130) to spatially align a previous non-attenuation corrected emission image (116) of the subject to the current non-attenuation corrected emission image of the subject; computing a CT reusability metric using at least one of (1) weight change of the subject between a time of acquisition of the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image and a time of acquisition of the current emission imaging data and (2) a relative overlap metric between the current non-attenuation corrected emission image of the subject and the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image spatially aligned using the spatial transformation; choosing method (i), (ii), or (iii) to generate an attenuation map based on the CT usability metric wherein: method (i) comprises warping a previous CT image (112) of the subject that is spatially aligned with the previous non-attenuation corrected emission image of the subject using the spatial transform to generate a warped CT image (142); method (ii) comprises warping the previous CT image (112) of the subject to generate the warped CT image (142), acquiring low dose CT imaging data (160) of the subject using the CT imaging system with an x-ray dose that is lower than the x-ray dose that was used to acquire the previous CT image, and reconstructing the low dose CT imaging data using the warped CT image as prior information to generate a low dose CT image (162); and 

 method (iii) comprises acquiring a new CT image of the subject after acquiring the current emission data; performing the chosen method to generate the attenuation map; and reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the generated attenuation map to generate an attenuation-corrected current emission image (148, 168) of the subject. 18. The non-transitory storage medium of claim 17 wherein the CT reusability metric is based on both (1) the weight change and (2) the relative overlap metric. 19. The non-transitory storage medium of any one of claims 17-18 wherein the CT reusability metric is computed using the weight change and is constructed such that a smaller weight change of the subject favors method (i) over method (ii) and favors method (ii) over method (iii). 20. The non-transitory storage medium of any one of claims 17-19 wherein the CT reusability metric uses the relative overlap metric and is constructed such that a higher relative overlap metric favors method (i) over method (ii) and favors method (ii) over method (iii) . 21. An imaging controller (10) comprising an electronic processor (12) programmed to perform a reduced radiation dose imaging method including: acquiring current emission imaging data (224) from a subject using an emission imaging system (6); acquiring a current CT image (222) of the subject using a CT imaging system (8); generating a spatial transform (230) to spatially align a previous CT image (112) to the current CT image of the subject; warping a previous attenuation corrected emission image (118) that is spatially aligned with the previous CT image using the spatial transform to generate a warped attenuation corrected emission image (234); and reconstructing the current emission imaging data with attenuation correction performed using the current CT image and using the warped attenuation corrected emission 

 image as prior data to generate an attenuation-corrected current emission image (238) of the subject. 22. The imaging controller of claim 21 wherein the previous CT image (112) is of the subject and the previous attenuation corrected emission image (118) is of the subject. 23. The imaging controller of claim 21 wherein the reduced radiation dose imaging method further includes: retrieving the previous CT image (112) and the previous attenuation corrected emission image (118) that is spatially aligned with the previous CT image from an emission/CT study atlas.
